mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in CPIAUCSL above 325 indicates persistent inflation, prompting central bank tightening and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or CPIAUCSL's predictive power fails in out-of-sample tests (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect collapses (p > 0.10 for joint coefficient) or 20d returns in expansionary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In high unemployment regimes, rising residential construction (RSAFS) reflects delayed housing market recovery, which fails to offset weak labor conditions, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of high-unemployment instances despite RSAFS growth, or interaction effect fails in out-of-sample tests (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates strong labor market conditions, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances with PAYEMS growth, or predictive power fails in out-of-sample tests (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1200 signals weak demand for new housing, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances with HOUST below 1200, or predictive power fails in out-of-sample tests (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and high inflation (CPIAUCSL) create a stagflationary environment, dampening corporate profits and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of stagflationary instances, or interaction effect fails in out-of-sample tests (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, rising GDP (GDPC1) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signal overheating economic conditions, prompting monetary tightening and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect collapses (p > 0.10 for joint coefficient) or 5d returns in late-cycle periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
